title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: The screen is calm against empty water. Brent held around $80.4–$80.6 even after Iran said the Strait was shut and Reuters reported only a single small tanker crossed; the market is pricing barrels that can still move through dark, state-linked and sanctions-tolerant channels, not proof that insured, transponder-on tonnage has returned.

CENTCOM counted 55 merchant ships and 17M+ barrels on Saturday and said Iran does not control the Strait, but a military denominator and an insured-commercial denominator are not the same object. AXS Marine put commercial crossings at 25 on 18 Jun vs roughly 120/day pre-war; PetroChina could not fix a VLCC near WS 650–750.

Range became a market variable. Russian-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales — fuel "only to government agencies" — after Ukrainian strikes; Zelensky said drones hit a Tyumen refinery more than 2,000 km away and claimed reach to 3,000 km, while Odesa-region exports risk falling from about 6mn to 4mn tons a month.

The Qwable leak sits outside the perimeter: the alleged Fable 5 distill is unproven, but the disputed trace set has spread to 29 related Hugging Face models. Access can be revoked, traces cannot — the access gate closes prospectively while the training trace cannot be untrained out of downstream weights.

props.title: Three Hormuz counts, three realities
props.subtitle: Strait transit reality · vessels per day
props.source: CENTCOM (55 merchant ships, 20 Jun), Reuters / AXS Marine (25 commercial crossings 18 Jun; ~120 pre-war), Reuters / Fars (no new permits after closure declared)

items.why: The test is not whether unattributable hulls keep slipping through; it is whether insured, transponder-on tonnage comes back as PGSA permits, war-risk premiums and the Lloyd's consortium settle.

items.what: Lake Lucerne working groups · written sequencing note
items.why: The room produced committees, not a sequencing term; the test is whether the working groups write custody, inspection and timing before the permit regime hardens into the framework's first real breach.

The UN Security Council has demanded the RSF halt an imminent offensive on a city the HRC says puts ~500,000 civilians at risk; the prevention window is open precisely because the full assault is not yet confirmed.

items.what: Crimea fuel rationing + Odesa export corridor
items.why: Every refinery interruption becomes market friction and every rationing order is evidence a strike crossed into administration, while Russian attacks could cut Odesa-region exports by up to a third.

The disputed Fable trace set has moved from one repo to 29 related models; the policy object is no longer a single artifact but a spreading downstream training input that no public takedown is visible against.

items.what: Lloyd's List reported war-risk premiums stepping up several-fold, with some ships at roughly 10 times pre-strike levels, and a new Hormuz marine war-risk consortium subject to underwriting and sanctions screening — a hull cleared by Iran can still be commercially blocked by insurance and finance.

Zelensky said Ukraine struck a Tyumen refinery — one of Russia's more modern, processing roughly 6 million tons of crude a year — more than 2,000 km away, and claimed modernized long-range drones can now reach 3,000 km; engines, navigation and warhead mass are now strategic variables.

A Qwen3.6 Fable5 LoRA was updated about ten hours before the file and the dataset card lists 4,665 trace sessions from 60 source sessions, but card-level attribution is not chain of custody and the quantitative benchmark status remains pending.
